summary: Cursor MCP-only: web_search -> write_file (to disk) -> read_file, streamed from store.db
review_criteria: proper formatting — clean segmentation, no run-on/garbled/merged text, no stray control chars or terminal escape codes
human-readable — reads like an assistant working, coherent and natural
correct text <-> tool interleaving order (narration then the tool it describes)
tool calls are the real intended tools (not leaked internal/shell noise where MCP tools were expected)
the final answer is coherent and matches the work performed
real work actually happened (e.g. the file was written to disk)

content_chunks:
I'll look up the workbench MCP tool schemas first, then run your three steps in order.
I'll search the web for the project code word.
The first search didn't connect; I'll retry the web search.
Next I'll save the code word to `result.txt`.
I'll read `result.txt` to confirm what was saved.
ZEBRA_e30c4eef
output.final:
I'll search the web for the project code word.

The first search didn't connect; I'll retry the web search.

workbench web_search

Next I'll save the code word to result.txt.

workbench write_file

I'll read result.txt to confirm what was saved.

workbench read_file

ZEBRA_e30c4eef
output.streamed_content:
I'll look up the workbench MCP tool schemas first, then run your three steps in order.
I'll search the web for the project code word.
The first search didn't connect; I'll retry the web search.
Next I'll save the code word to `result.txt`.

issues: Cursor exposes MCP tools via its meta-tools GetMcpTools/CallMcpTool, so streamed tool names are the wrappers, not the underlying web_search/write_file/read_file (the narration names the real action). Cosmetic, not a streaming defect.
agent_review.notes: After the store.db-selection fix (re-resolve freshest post-baseline store.db, skipping cursor's warmup 'OK' turns), cursor streams the real turn: 6 readable narration chunks + 5 tool starts, correctly interleaved (text->tool x5), no duplication, and result.txt written to disk with the code word. GOOD.
